visit_summary: Avoid. A prominent white stress fracture is visible on the load-bearing plastic base, posing a structural risk.
alignment_summary: The listing describes the cart as being in 'fair condition,' but the photo reveals a prominent white stress fracture or bend mark on the load-bearing teal plastic base near the rear wheel.

mismatches.issue: Structural stress fracture on the plastic base

reasons.text: A prominent white stress mark/fracture is visible on the teal plastic base near the wheel, indicating structural weakness.
The description claims 'fair condition' but fails to mention the structural stress damage on the load-bearing frame.
Structural damage on a toddler push-toy poses a collapse or tipping risk during active play.

seller_questions: Is the white mark on the teal base a crack or a deep stress bend in the plastic?
Does the cart wobble or lean when weight is placed on the handle?
Are all four wheels spinning smoothly and securely attached?
seller_message_draft: Hi! Is this still available? I noticed the white mark on the teal base in the photo—is that a crack or a deep bend in the plastic? Just want to make sure it's sturdy enough for my toddler to push. Thanks!
limitations: We cannot verify the stability of the wheels or axle alignment from a single photo.
The exact dimensions and brand of the shopping cart are not specified in the text.
We cannot test the strength of the stressed plastic under actual weight or play conditions.
research_recommended: Search for common plastic toy shopping cart recalls involving tipping or handle breakage.
Compare dimensions of standard toddler shopping carts to ensure it is the right height for your child.
future_capability_note: We don't verify prices, recalls, or seller history yet — you'll need to check these yourself. A future version of this product will do that research for you.
